input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(examples): add I2C DS1307 Read demo

Bit-bang i2c master reads seconds/minutes/hours from the DS1307
real-time clock at addr 0x68 and prints "HH:MM:SS\n" to UART.
Inlines i2c primitives (same shape as i2c_add1_ping.s) and adds
print_bcd_byte for BCD → ASCII formatting; CH (sec bit 7) and
12/24-mode (hr bit 6) masked before decode.

Read sequence per the DS1307 datasheet (and the emulator device's
register doc): start → write 0xD0 (addr+W) → write 0x00 (pointer)
→ restart → write 0xD1 (addr+R) → read S → read M → read H → stop.
Pointer auto-increments per read.

Pair with cor24-emu --i2c-device ds1307@0x68. The CLI spec
accepts no params, so out-of-the-box the demo prints "00:00:00\n"
(default all-zero registers). The web side's planned i2c-rtc-panel
will use Ds1307HandleExt::set_time(...) to drive non-zero values
for the live demo.

Without an i2c device attached, the emulator's bus returns 0 for
SDA reads under the new (post-DS1307) open-drain default, so the
output is indistinguishable from the attached-but-zero case — also
"00:00:00\n". Either way the program halts cleanly.

Registered in tests/integration_tests.rs::examples() between
"I2C Add1 Ping" and "Literals". 1177 instructions end-to-end.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Saga shipped — both pr/ branches ready for relay, no stranded commits.

---

## Now the new request: time-setting paths

You said: "emulator preset from system clock (as an option) **OR** demo asks the user to set the time via UART". These map to two different repos:

| Option | What | Repo | Who | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **A. Emulator system-clock preset** | New CLI param like `ds1307@0x68?preset=system` (or `?hour=12&min=34&sec=56` for explicit) that initializes the device's BCD registers from the host clock at attach time. Set-once at startup. | sw-cor24-emulator (`registry.rs::build_i2c_device` + a setter on `Ds1307Device`) | **dcemu** brief (I can draft, can't ship) | small — ~50 lines + tests |
| **B. UART-driven time-set demo** | New `.s` example (or extend `i2c_ds1307_read.s`) that reads `HHMMSS` from UART RX, parses to BCD, writes registers via i2c, then reads back and prints. Interactive each run. | sw-cor24-x-assembler (this repo) | **dcxas** saga (me) — can ship today | medium — ~250 lines (more i2c-writes for setting all 3 registers, plus UART RX parsing) |

They're complementary, not exclusive. The realistic live-demo flow likely wants **both**: A gives the demo a sensible starting time without UI; B lets users prove the round-trip without needing the web panel.

A few observations on B specifically:
- UART RX in this emulator: I haven't yet exercised it. Need to check the contract (there's a `--uart-input` flag and bit semantics on `0xFF0101`).
- "Set via UART" implies prompting + parsing. Simplest spec: read exactly 6 ASCII digits, no separators, set H=digits[0:2] M=[2:4] S=[4:6]. Reject non-digit gracefully (or just halt).

toolUseResult.content:
# Saga: i2c-ds1307-set

## Goal

Add `src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s` — interactive
DS1307 demo. Reads 6 ASCII digits (`HHMMSS`) from UART RX,
parses them to BCD, writes the DS1307 registers via i2c, then
reads them back and prints `HH:MM:SS\n` to verify the round-trip.
Halts.

Pairs with the existing `i2c_ds1307_read.s` (already on
pr/i2c-ds1307-read awaiting mike's relay):
- **Read demo:** prints whatever the registers currently hold
  (default `00:00:00` without a setter).
- **Set demo (this saga):** lets the user (or `--uart-input`)
  drive the time interactively, then reads it back.

Future RTC demos noted but out of scope here:
- **count-up timer** (separate saga) — set RTC to zero, spin-loop
  poll, print every 1s.
- **system-clock preset** — dcemu is working on the emulator-side
  CLI param `?preset=system` (or similar). When that lands, a
  third "show current host time" demo becomes trivial (no setup
  needed; read demo already covers it).

## What's actually changing

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s` (new) | UART RX → 6-digit ASCII parse → BCD → i2c-write registers 0–2 → i2c-read back → print "HH:MM:SS\n" |
| `tests/integration_tests.rs` (2 hunks) | register `("I2C DS1307 Set", ...)` between "I2C DS1307 Read" (when that lands) and "Literals". Note: this branch is based off origin/dev which doesn't yet have the Read entry — alphabetical insertion goes between "I2C Add1 Ping" and "Literals". Mike's two merges (Read then Set) should sequence cleanly. |

## UART RX contract (per `sw-cor24-emulator/src/cpu/state.rs:55`)

- `0xFF0101` status bits:
    - bit 0 = RX data ready
    - bit 2 = RX overflow
    - bit 7 = TX busy (already used by putc)
- `0xFF0100` data: read for RX (auto-clears ready), write for TX
- Test path: `cor24-emu --uart-input "123456" --i2c-device ds1307@0x68`

`uart_getc`: poll bit 0 of status, then read data. Returns char
in r0.

## Architectural note

Same fp-frame addressing pattern as i2c_add1_ping / i2c_ds1307_read.
Inlines its own copy of all i2c primitives, putc, print_hex_nibble,
print_bcd_byte (no `.include` in this assembler).

For BCD parse: each pair of ASCII digits → BCD byte via
`(d0 - '0') << 4 | (d1 - '0')`. No bounds checking on input
digits — garbage in, garbage out (the test path injects clean
input via `--uart-input`).

- i2cstart → write 0xD0 (addr+W) → write 0x00 (pointer) →
  write seconds → write minutes → write hours → i2cstop
- Pointer auto-increments per write byte.

After the write, the read-back is identical to the read demo:
i2cstart → write 0xD0 → write 0x00 → restart → write 0xD1 →
read S → read M → read H → i2cstop → format + print.

## Out of scope

- No prompt printed before reading (program just blocks on UART
  RX). Keeps the demo minimal; `--uart-input` automates it.
- No input validation (non-digit chars produce garbage output).
- No count-up timer (separate saga).
- No `cor24-emulator` changes — DS1307 device is shipped at
  origin/dev 0eed0e1 / origin/main 8d12b75 (Release 0eed0e1).
- No system-clock preset — dcemu's parallel work.
- No web `i2c-rtc-panel` — separate dwxas saga.

input.content:
# Saga: i2c-ds1307-set

## Goal

Add `src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s` — interactive
DS1307 demo. Reads 6 ASCII digits (`HHMMSS`) from UART RX,
parses them to BCD, writes the DS1307 registers via i2c, then
reads them back and prints `HH:MM:SS\n` to verify the round-trip.
Halts.

Pairs with the existing `i2c_ds1307_read.s` (already on
pr/i2c-ds1307-read awaiting mike's relay):
- **Read demo:** prints whatever the registers currently hold
  (default `00:00:00` without a setter).
- **Set demo (this saga):** lets the user (or `--uart-input`)
  drive the time interactively, then reads it back.

Future RTC demos noted but out of scope here:
- **count-up timer** (separate saga) — set RTC to zero, spin-loop
  poll, print every 1s.
- **system-clock preset** — dcemu is working on the emulator-side
  CLI param `?preset=system` (or similar). When that lands, a
  third "show current host time" demo becomes trivial (no setup
  needed; read demo already covers it).

## What's actually changing

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s` (new) | UART RX → 6-digit ASCII parse → BCD → i2c-write registers 0–2 → i2c-read back → print "HH:MM:SS\n" |
| `tests/integration_tests.rs` (2 hunks) | register `("I2C DS1307 Set", ...)` between "I2C DS1307 Read" (when that lands) and "Literals". Note: this branch is based off origin/dev which doesn't yet have the Read entry — alphabetical insertion goes between "I2C Add1 Ping" and "Literals". Mike's two merges (Read then Set) should sequence cleanly. |

## UART RX contract (per `sw-cor24-emulator/src/cpu/state.rs:55`)

- `0xFF0101` status bits:
    - bit 0 = RX data ready
    - bit 2 = RX overflow
    - bit 7 = TX busy (already used by putc)
- `0xFF0100` data: read for RX (auto-clears ready), write for TX
- Test path: `cor24-emu --uart-input "123456" --i2c-device ds1307@0x68`

`uart_getc`: poll bit 0 of status, then read data. Returns char
in r0.

## Architectural note

Same fp-frame addressing pattern as i2c_add1_ping / i2c_ds1307_read.
Inlines its own copy of all i2c primitives, putc, print_hex_nibble,
print_bcd_byte (no `.include` in this assembler).

For BCD parse: each pair of ASCII digits → BCD byte via
`(d0 - '0') << 4 | (d1 - '0')`. No bounds checking on input
digits — garbage in, garbage out (the test path injects clean
input via `--uart-input`).

Write sequence (per DS1307 datasheet + emulator's device doc):
- i2cstart → write 0xD0 (addr+W) → write 0x00 (pointer) →
  write seconds → write minutes → write hours → i2cstop
- Pointer auto-increments per write byte.

After the write, the read-back is identical to the read demo:
i2cstart → write 0xD0 → write 0x00 → restart → write 0xD1 →
read S → read M → read H → i2cstop → format + print.

## Out of scope

- No prompt printed before reading (program just blocks on UART
  RX). Keeps the demo minimal; `--uart-input` automates it.
- No input validation (non-digit chars produce garbage output).
- No count-up timer (separate saga).
- No `cor24-emulator` changes — DS1307 device is shipped at
  origin/dev 0eed0e1 / origin/main 8d12b75 (Release 0eed0e1).
- No system-clock preset — dcemu's parallel work.
- No web `i2c-rtc-panel` — separate dwxas saga.

## Steps

1. **i2c-ds1307-set** — single step. Write the .s, register in
   tests, verify with cor24-emu + --uart-input + ds1307, commit.

## When done

Two pr/ branches per the established pattern:
- `pr/i2c-ds1307-set` — the work
- `pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete` — bookkeeping

The pending `pr/i2c-ds1307-read` should land first (mike's
choice); if Set lands first the tests/integration_tests.rs hunks
will need a one-line manual reconcile by mike (both insert in
alphabetical position; trivial).

toolUseResult.content:
Write `src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s`: read 6 ASCII
digits from UART RX (`HHMMSS`), parse to BCD, write the DS1307
seconds/minutes/hours registers via i2c, then read back and
print `HH:MM:SS\n`. Halt.

## UART RX contract

- `0xFF0101` status bit 0 = RX data ready
- `0xFF0100` data: read auto-clears ready

`uart_getc` (new subroutine; mirrors putc's structure):

```
uart_getc:
        push    r1
        la      r1, -65279      ; UART status = 0xFF0101
.ug_wait:
        lb      r2, 0(r1)
        lcu     r0, 1           ; RX-ready mask
        and     r2, r0
        ceq     r2, z
        brt     .ug_wait
        la      r1, -65280      ; UART data = 0xFF0100
        lbu     r0, 0(r1)       ; read clears ready
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)
```

(Adjust to use the established calling convention — `r1` is
return addr, push at entry, pop+jmp on exit.)

## Read 2 ASCII digits → 1 BCD byte

`read_bcd_pair`: call uart_getc twice, build
`((d0 - '0') << 4) | (d1 - '0')` into r0.

```
read_bcd_pair:
        push    r1
        ; first digit (tens)
        la      r1, .rbp_r1
        la      r2, uart_getc
        jal     r1, (r2)
.rbp_r1:
        add     r0, -48         ; r0 -= '0'  → 0..9
        push    r0              ; tens digit on stack
        ; second digit (ones)
        la      r1, .rbp_r2
        la      r2, uart_getc
        jal     r1, (r2)
.rbp_r2:
        add     r0, -48
        ; combine: (tens << 4) | ones
        pop     r2              ; r2 = tens
        lc      r1, 4
        shl     r2, r1
        or      r0, r2
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)
```

## Main flow

```
        ; init stack
        la      r0, 0FEEC00h
        mov     sp, r0

        ; read 3 BCD pairs: H, M, S
        la      r1, .ret_h
        la      r2, read_bcd_pair
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_h:
        push    r0              ; hours on stack

        la      r1, .ret_m
        la      r2, read_bcd_pair
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_m:
        push    r0              ; minutes

        la      r1, .ret_s
        la      r2, read_bcd_pair
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_s:
        push    r0              ; seconds

        ; --- write registers via i2c ---
        ; sp+0 = seconds  (most recently pushed)
        ; sp+3 = minutes
        ; sp+6 = hours
        ; Use fp as frame anchor so the offsets stay stable through nested calls.
        push    fp
        mov     fp, sp
        ; fp+0 = saved fp
        ; fp+3 = seconds
        ; fp+6 = minutes
        ; fp+9 = hours

        ; i2cstart, write 0xD0, write 0x00 (pointer), write S, write M, write H, i2cstop
        ... (use the jal/return pattern with appropriate ret labels)

        ; --- read back ---
        ; i2cstart, write 0xD0, write 0x00, restart, write 0xD1,
        ; read S' → push, read M' → push, read H' → push, i2cstop

        ; --- print "HH:MM:SS\n" ---
        ; Use print_bcd_byte with the masks from the read demo (0x3F for hours,
        ; 0x7F for seconds). Print H, ':', M, ':', S, '\n'.

        ; --- teardown ---
        mov     sp, fp
        pop     fp
        add     sp, 9           ; discard the original H, M, S input slots
halt:
        bra     halt
```

## Reuse from i2c_ds1307_read.s (copy verbatim)

- `putc` (UART write)
- `print_hex_nibble` (handles BCD 0-9 via '+0' path)
- `print_bcd_byte` (uses print_hex_nibble for both nibbles)
- `i2cstart`, `i2cstop`, `i2cwrite`, `i2cread` (the bit-bang
  primitives with fp-frame addressing)

These took several rounds to get right — copy exactly.

## Verify

```bash
target/release/cor24-asm src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s -o /tmp/dsset.lgo
EMU=/disk1/.../sw-cor24-emulator/target/release/cor24-emu

# Set 12:34:56 via UART input
$EMU --lgo /tmp/dsset.lgo --uart-input "123456" \
     --i2c-device ds1307@0x68 --quiet --time 5

# expect: "12:34:56\n"
```

Also:
```bash
cargo test --workspace
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
```

## Register in tests

Add to `tests/integration_tests.rs::examples()` alphabetically.
Note this branch is based off origin/dev which does NOT yet have
the "I2C DS1307 Read" entry (that's on pending pr/i2c-ds1307-read).
So this insertion goes between "I2C Add1 Ping" and "Literals" —
same place the Read demo's PR will insert its entry. Mike's
sequencing handles the merge: if Read lands first (likely), Set
inserts cleanly after Read; if Set lands first, Read inserts
before Set on its next merge.

## test_all_examples_halt note

The example WILL HANG without a `--uart-input` because uart_getc
busy-polls forever waiting for input. To keep
`test_all_examples_halt` happy, add `"I2C DS1307 Set"` to the
`non_halting` array in that test (same shape as "Button Echo",
which loops forever).

## Commit

```
feat(examples): add I2C DS1307 Set demo (UART-driven time set)

Reads 6 ASCII digits (HHMMSS) from UART RX, parses to BCD,
writes the DS1307 seconds/minutes/hours registers via i2c, then
reads back and prints HH:MM:SS\n. Halt.

Pairs with i2c_ds1307_read.s: read = pure observer (default
0:00:00 without a setter); set = interactive driver that proves
the i2c write path through the same bit-bang library. Together
they exercise both directions of the DS1307 protocol.

Future demos noted but out of scope: count-up timer (zero +
spin-poll); system-clock preset (waits on dcemu's emulator-side
work).

Verified via cor24-emu --uart-input "123456" --i2c-device
ds1307@0x68 producing "12:34:56\n".

Registered in tests/integration_tests.rs::examples() and added
to non_halting (uart_getc busy-polls without input).
```

## Wrap

`agentrail complete --done`. `dg-mark-pr` → `pr/i2c-ds1307-set`.
Post-complete bookkeeping branch → `pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete`.
STOP.

;     # expect "12:34:56\n"
;
; Without --uart-input the program busy-polls UART RX forever waiting for
; the 6 digits — that's the interactive case (use --terminal). The
; tests/integration_tests.rs harness handles this by adding the example to
; the `non_halting` list.
;
; MMIO:
;     0xFF0020 = SCL ; 0xFF0021 = SDA (open-drain bit-bang)
;     0xFF0100 = UART data (read = RX, write = TX; read auto-clears RX-ready)
;     0xFF0101 = UART status:
;                  bit 0 = RX ready, bit 2 = RX overflow, bit 7 = TX busy
;
; DS1307 register write sequence (auto-incrementing pointer):
;     i2cstart → write 0xD0 (addr+W) → write 0x00 (pointer = Seconds) →
;     write S → write M → write H → i2cstop
;
; Read-back follows the same shape as i2c_ds1307_read.s.
;
; Bit-bang i2c primitives, putc, print_hex_nibble, and print_bcd_byte are
; copied verbatim from i2c_ds1307_read.s — this assembler has no .include
; or macro mechanism.

        ; --- main ---
        la      r0, 0FEEC00h
        mov     sp, r0

        ; Read 3 BCD pairs from UART: H, M, S
        la      r1, .ret_rh
        la      r2, read_bcd_pair
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rh:
        push    r0              ; hours (most-recent push at sp+0)

        la      r1, .ret_rm
        la      r2, read_bcd_pair
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rm:
        push    r0              ; minutes

        la      r1, .ret_rs
        la      r2, read_bcd_pair
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rs:
        push    r0              ; seconds

        ; Stack now: sp+0=S, sp+3=M, sp+6=H
        ; Pin an fp frame so the offsets stay stable through nested calls.
        push    fp
        mov     fp, sp          ; fp+0 = saved fp; +3=S, +6=M, +9=H

        ; --- i2c write: set pointer = 0, then S, M, H ---
        la      r1, .ret_ws1
        la      r2, i2cstart
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ws1:
        lcu     r0, 0D0h        ; addr 0x68 << 1, W
        la      r1, .ret_ww1
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ww1:
        lc      r0, 0           ; pointer = 0
        la      r1, .ret_ww2
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ww2:
        lw      r0, 3(fp)       ; seconds
        la      r1, .ret_wws
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_wws:
        lw      r0, 6(fp)       ; minutes
        la      r1, .ret_wwm
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_wwm:
        lw      r0, 9(fp)       ; hours
        la      r1, .ret_wwh
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_wwh:
        la      r1, .ret_ws2
        la      r2, i2cstop
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ws2:

        ; --- i2c read-back: pointer = 0, then read S', M', H' ---
        la      r1, .ret_rs1
        la      r2, i2cstart
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rs1:
        lcu     r0, 0D0h
        la      r1, .ret_rw1
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rw1:
        lc      r0, 0
        la      r1, .ret_rw2
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rw2:
        la      r1, .ret_rs2
        la      r2, i2cstart
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rs2:
        lcu     r0, 0D1h        ; addr 0x68 << 1, R
        la      r1, .ret_rw3
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rw3:
        la      r1, .ret_rrs
        la      r2, i2cread
        jal     r1, (r2)        ; r0 = seconds (read-back)
.ret_rrs:
        push    r0              ; new local: S' at sp+0 (offsets from fp shift)
        la      r1, .ret_rrm
        la      r2, i2cread
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rrm:
        push    r0              ; M' at sp+0; S' at sp+3
        la      r1, .ret_rrh
        la      r2, i2cread
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rrh:
        push    r0              ; H' at sp+0; M' at sp+3; S' at sp+6
        la      r1, .ret_rs3
        la      r2, i2cstop
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rs3:

        ; --- print "HH:MM:SS\n" from the read-back values ---
        ; They're on the stack at sp+0/+3/+6 (H'/M'/S'). Pop them as we print.
        pop     r0              ; H'
        lcu     r2, 3Fh
        and     r0, r2          ; mask 12/24-mode bit
        la      r1, .ret_ph
        la      r2, print_bcd_byte
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ph:
        lc      r0, 58          ; ':'
        la      r1, .ret_c1
        la      r2, putc
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_c1:
        pop     r0              ; M'
        la      r1, .ret_pm
        la      r2, print_bcd_byte
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_pm:
        lc      r0, 58          ; ':'
        la      r1, .ret_c2
        la      r2, putc
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_c2:
        pop     r0              ; S'
        lcu     r2, 7Fh
        and     r0, r2          ; mask CH bit
        la      r1, .ret_ps
        la      r2, print_bcd_byte
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ps:
        lc      r0, 10          ; '\n'
        la      r1, .ret_nl
        la      r2, putc
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_nl:

        ; --- teardown: drop the fp frame + the 3 original input slots ---
        mov     sp, fp
        pop     fp
        add     sp, 9           ; discard H, M, S input slots (3 × 3 bytes)

halt:
        bra     halt

; ============================================================================
; uart_getc(): block until UART RX has a byte, then return it in r0.
; ============================================================================

uart_getc:
        push    r1
        la      r1, -65279      ; UART status = 0xFF0101
.ug_wait:
        lbu     r2, 0(r1)
        lcu     r0, 1           ; RX-ready mask (bit 0)
        and     r2, r0
        ceq     r2, z
        brt     .ug_wait
        la      r1, -65280      ; UART data = 0xFF0100  (read clears RX-ready)
        lbu     r0, 0(r1)
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

; ============================================================================
; read_bcd_pair(): read 2 ASCII digits from UART, return BCD byte in r0.
;                  r0 = ((d0 - '0') << 4) | (d1 - '0')
; ============================================================================

read_bcd_pair:
        push    r1
        ; tens digit
        la      r1, .rbp_r1
        la      r2, uart_getc
        jal     r1, (r2)
.rbp_r1:
        add     r0, -48         ; ASCII → 0..9
        push    r0
        ; ones digit
        la      r1, .rbp_r2
        la      r2, uart_getc
        jal     r1, (r2)
.rbp_r2:
        add     r0, -48
        pop     r2              ; r2 = tens
        lc      r1, 4
        shl     r2, r1
        or      r0, r2
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

; ============================================================================
; print_bcd_byte(r0): emit r0 (low 8 bits) as 2 BCD digits.
; ============================================================================

print_bcd_byte:
        push    r1
        push    r0
        lc      r1, 4
        srl     r0, r1          ; upper nibble
        la      r1, .pbb_ret1
        la      r2, print_hex_nibble
        jal     r1, (r2)
.pbb_ret1:
        pop     r0
        lcu     r2, 0Fh
        and     r0, r2          ; lower nibble
        la      r1, .pbb_ret2
        la      r2, print_hex_nibble
        jal     r1, (r2)
.pbb_ret2:
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

; ============================================================================
; UART TX helpers (copied verbatim from i2c_ds1307_read.s)
; ============================================================================

putc:
        push    r1
        push    r0
        la      r1, -65280
.putc_wait:
        lb      r2, 1(r1)
        cls     r2, z
        brt     .putc_wait
        pop     r0
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

print_hex_nibble:
        push    r1
        lcu     r2, 10
        clu     r0, r2
        brt     .phn_digit
        add     r0, 55
        bra     .phn_emit
.phn_digit:
        add     r0, 48
.phn_emit:
        la      r2, putc
        jal     r1, (r2)
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

; ============================================================================
; I2C bit-bang primitives (copied verbatim from i2c_ds1307_read.s)
; ============================================================================

i2cstart:
        push    r1
        la      r1, -65504
        lcu     r0, 1
        sb      r0, 1(r1)
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        lc      r0, 0
        sb      r0, 1(r1)
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

i2cstop:
        push    r1
        la      r1, -65504
        lc      r0, 0
        sb      r0, 1(r1)
        lcu     r0, 1
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        sb      r0, 1(r1)
        lc      r0, 0
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

i2cwrite:
        push    r1
        push    fp
        push    r0
        mov     fp, sp
        lcu     r2, 80h
.iw_loop:
        lbu     r0, 0(fp)
        and     r0, r2
        ceq     r0, z
        brt     .iw_zero
        lc      r0, 1
        bra     .iw_set
.iw_zero:
        lc      r0, 0
.iw_set:
        la      r1, -65504
        sb      r0, 1(r1)
        lcu     r0, 1
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        lc      r0, 0
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        lc      r1, 1
        srl     r2, r1
        ceq     r2, z
        brf     .iw_loop
        la      r1, -65504
        lcu     r0, 1
        sb      r0, 1(r1)
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        lbu     r0, 1(r1)
        lcu     r2, 1
        and     r0, r2
        push    r0
        lc      r2, 0
        la      r1, -65504
        sb      r2, 0(r1)
        pop     r0
        add     sp, 3
        pop     fp
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

i2cread:
        push    r1
        push    fp
        lc      r0, 0
        push    r0
        mov     fp, sp
        lcu     r2, 8
.ir_loop:
        la      r1, -65504
        lcu     r0, 1
        sb      r0, 1(r1)
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        lbu     r0, 1(r1)
        push    r0
        lw      r0, 0(fp)
        add     r0, r0
        pop     r1
        or      r0, r1
        sw      r0, 0(fp)
        la      r1, -65504
        lc      r0, 0
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        add     r2, -1
        ceq     r2, z
        brf     .ir_loop
        la      r1, -65504
        lc      r0, 0
        sb      r0, 1(r1)
        lcu     r0, 1
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        lc      r0, 0
        sb      r0, 0(r1)
        pop     r0
        pop     fp
        pop     r1
        jmp     (r1)

;
; Reads 6 ASCII digits (HHMMSS) from UART RX, parses to BCD, writes the
; DS1307's seconds/minutes/hours registers (0x00-0x02) via i2c, then reads
; the registers back and prints "HH:MM:SS\n" to UART. Halts.
;
; Pair with `cor24-emu --uart-input "<6 digits>" --i2c-device ds1307@0x68`:
;     cor24-asm src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s -o /tmp/dsset.lgo
;     cor24-emu --lgo /tmp/dsset.lgo --uart-input "123456" \
;               --i2c-device ds1307@0x68 --quiet

; the `non_halting` list.

;     i2cstart → write 0xD0 (addr+W) → write 0x00 (pointer = Seconds) →
;     write S → write M → write H → i2cstop

; Read-back follows the same shape as i2c_ds1307_read.s.

; Bit-bang i2c primitives, putc, print_hex_nibble, and print_bcd_byte are
; copied verbatim from i2c_ds1307_read.s — this assembler has no .include
; or macro mechanism.

        ; --- main ---

        mov     sp, r0

        ; Read 3 BCD pairs from UART: H, M, S

        la      r2, read_bcd_pair
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rh:
        push    r0              ; hours (most-recent push at sp+0)

        la      r1, .ret_rm
        la      r2, read_bcd_pair
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rm:
        push    r0              ; minutes

        la      r1, .ret_rs

.ret_rs:

        push    fp

        ; --- i2c write: set pointer = 0, then S, M, H ---
        la      r1, .ret_ws1
        la      r2, i2cstart
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ws1:
        lcu     r0, 0D0h        ; addr 0x68 << 1, W
        la      r1, .ret_ww1
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ww1:
        lc      r0, 0           ; pointer = 0
        la      r1, .ret_ww2
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ww2:
        lw      r0, 3(fp)       ; seconds
        la      r1, .ret_wws
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_wws:
        lw      r0, 6(fp)       ; minutes
        la      r1, .ret_wwm
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_wwm:
        lw      r0, 9(fp)       ; hours
        la      r1, .ret_wwh
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_wwh:
        la      r1, .ret_ws2
        la      r2, i2cstop
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ws2:

        ; --- i2c read-back: pointer = 0, then read S', M', H' ---
        la      r1, .ret_rs1
        la      r2, i2cstart
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rs1:
        lcu     r0, 0D0h
        la      r1, .ret_rw1
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rw1:
        lc      r0, 0
        la      r1, .ret_rw2
        la      r2, i2cwrite
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rw2:

        la      r2, i2cstart
        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_rs2:

        la      r2, print_bcd_byte

.ret_pm:
        lc      r0, 58          ; ':'

        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_c2:
        pop     r0              ; S'
        lcu     r2, 7Fh
        and     r0, r2          ; mask CH bit

        jal     r1, (r2)
.ret_ps:

git add src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s tests/integration_tests.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(examples): add I2C DS1307 Set demo (UART-driven time set)

Reads 6 ASCII digits (HHMMSS) from UART RX, parses to BCD,
writes the DS1307 seconds/minutes/hours registers (0x00-0x02)
via i2c using the auto-incrementing pointer protocol, then
reads them back and prints HH:MM:SS\n to verify the round-trip.

Pairs with i2c_ds1307_read.s: read = pure observer (default
0:00:00 without a setter); set = interactive driver that proves
the i2c write path through the same bit-bang library. Together
they exercise both directions of the DS1307 protocol.

Future RTC demos noted but out of scope:
  - count-up timer (zero + spin-poll + 1s heartbeat)
  - system-clock preset (waits on dcemu's emulator-side init path)
  - battery-backed RTC (dcemu is analyzing the design)

Verified via cor24-emu --uart-input <6-digits> --i2c-device
ds1307@0x68: 12:34:56, 09:30:00, 23:59:59 all round-trip cleanly
in ~8K instructions per run.

Registered in tests/integration_tests.rs::examples() alphabetically
and added to non_halting in test_all_examples_halt (uart_getc
busy-polls UART RX forever without input).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
All four pr/* branches ready:

- **`pr/i2c-ds1307-read`** (3 commits) — read demo + RTC label rename
- **`pr/i2c-ds1307-read-saga-complete`** — bookkeeping
- **`pr/i2c-ds1307-set`** (3 commits) — set demo + RTC label rename
- **`pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete`** — bookkeeping

No stranded commits. Dropdown labels are now `"I2C RTC Read"` / `"I2C RTC Set"`. DS1307 stays in code comments / filenames / source itself.

---

**Quick take on the battery-backed RTC analysis you asked for** (this is dcemu's design, just my reading):

**Power-on default = 0:00:00** matches DS1307 datasheet semantics (CH bit cleared, registers zero on first power), so keeping it as the default is right.

**"With battery" simulation** has a clean split that maps to where state lives:
- **Emulator side** stays *stateless across runs* — just exposes a constructor like `Ds1307Device::with_initial_time(Hms)` and (when system-clock preset lands) `Ds1307Device::with_system_clock()`. CLI gets `?hour=X&minute=Y&second=Z` plus `?preset=system`. No persistence in the emulator itself.
- **Web side owns persistence.** localStorage entry `{ set_value: { h, m, s }, set_at_ms: <Date.now()> }`. On each page load:
  - `elapsed_s = (Date.now() - set_at_ms) / 1000`
  - `effective = (set_value as seconds) + elapsed_s` mod 24h
  - Pass `effective` to `Ds1307Device::with_initial_time(...)` at attach time
- That gives "battery survives page reload AND clock continues ticking while page was closed" with zero emulator-side persistence machinery.

**Two web demos fall out naturally**: a "no battery" toggle (always pass 0:00:00) and a "with battery" toggle (use localStorage). Same .s file (read demo) drives both — the difference is just the constructor args.

**One trap to flag in the brief**: if the web demo lets the user *set* the time mid-session (via the set demo or a UI slider), localStorage must be updated synchronously with the i2c-write transaction completion. Otherwise on next reload the persisted value is stale by the duration of the current run. dcemu doesn't need to handle this; it's a web-side discipline.

**Naming**: I'd call the emulator-side knobs "initial registers" not "battery" — it keeps the device device-shaped and lets the web layer own the battery metaphor. Same way `tmp101@0x4A?temp=25.0` doesn't pretend to be a thermistor.

Want me to write that up as a dcemu brief?

message.content: yes, write the dcemu brief

content.text: Drafting the brief now.

input.content:
# Brief: DS1307 initial-time CLI params + system-clock preset

**Owner:** dcemu
**Branch:** `pr/ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset`
**Repo:** `sw-cor24-x-assembler`'s emulator path-dep — `sw-cor24-emulator`
**Drafted by:** dcxas (2026-05-16)

## Why this brief exists

dcxas just shipped two RTC demos in the assembler repo:
`i2c_ds1307_read.s` (passive observer, prints `HH:MM:SS\n`) and
`i2c_ds1307_set.s` (UART-driven setter that writes the registers
via i2c then reads them back). Both work end-to-end against
`cor24-emu --i2c-device ds1307@0x68` — but **the CLI spec accepts
no params**, so out-of-the-box the read demo prints `00:00:00`
forever. The only way to get a non-zero starting time today is
the in-Rust `Ds1307HandleExt::set_*` API (which the web panel
will use, but is unavailable to CLI users).

This brief proposes the missing CLI surface so:

- dcxas's read demo can show meaningful output from a one-liner
  without the web panel (`--i2c-device 'ds1307@0x68?preset=system'`
  shows the host clock).
- dwxas's `web-sw-cor24-x-assembler` battery-backed RTC demo
  (currently in design — see "Battery story" below) has a clean
  emulator-side hook to pass an effective initial time without
  routing through `handle.set_time`.
- The "no battery" vs "with battery" web toggle becomes a one-arg
  CLI-side difference rather than two separate Rust code paths.

## What changes

Add three things to `sw-cor24-emulator`:

1. **`Ds1307Device::with_initial_registers(...)`** — a public
   constructor that takes the 8 BCD register values (S/M/H/DoW/
   Date/Month/Year/Control) and pre-loads them. Defaults match
   `new()` (all-zero, control = 0). Use it in `new()` itself for
   the existing "all-zero" path, so there's one source of truth.
2. **Registry parser support for `ds1307@<addr>?<params>`** — in
   `src/peripherals/i2c/registry.rs::build_i2c_device`'s `"ds1307"`
   arm, accept these param keys (all optional):

   | Key      | Value         | Behavior                                  |
   |----------|---------------|-------------------------------------------|
   | `hour`   | 0-23 decimal  | Sets the Hours register (BCD-encoded; 24-hour). Conflicts with `preset`. |
   | `minute` | 0-59 decimal  | Sets Minutes. Conflicts with `preset`.     |
   | `second` | 0-59 decimal  | Sets Seconds. Conflicts with `preset`.     |
   | `date`   | 1-31 decimal  | Sets Date register. Conflicts with `preset`. |
   | `month`  | 1-12 decimal  | Sets Month. Conflicts with `preset`.       |
   | `year`   | 0-99 decimal  | Sets Year register (2-digit). Conflicts with `preset`. |
   | `dow`    | 1-7 decimal   | Sets DayOfWeek register. Conflicts with `preset`. |
   | `preset` | `system`      | Reads host clock at attach time and sets all 7 time registers from it. Conflicts with any of the above keys. |

   Reject unknown keys (current pattern). Reject out-of-range
   values with a clear error message naming both the key and the
   value (`"ds1307 'hour' out of range: 24 (valid: 0-23)"`).
   Reject `preset` with `?hour=` etc. set
   (`"ds1307 'preset' and explicit register values are mutually exclusive"`).

3. **Help text update** in the CLI:

   ```
   --i2c-device <spec>    Attach an I2C device (repeatable). Specs:
                            add1@<addr>[?wrap=<n>]                universal +1 test slave
                            tmp101@<addr>[?temp=<f>][?config=<n>] TI temp sensor
                            ds1307@<addr>[?hour=<n>][?minute=<n>]
                                  [?second=<n>][?date=<n>][?month=<n>]
                                  [?year=<n>][?dow=<n>]
                                  [?preset=system]                Dallas/Maxim RTC
   ```

That's the entire surface change. The existing
`Ds1307HandleExt::set_time / set_date / tick_second / ...` runtime
API stays as-is (it's what the web panel's slider will use for
mid-session updates).

## Tests

Add to `src/peripherals/i2c/registry.rs` test module:

- `build_ds1307_with_time_params` — `ds1307@0x68?hour=12&minute=34&second=56`
  → device address 0x68, register 0x00 = 0x56 (BCD), 0x01 = 0x34,
  0x02 = 0x12.
- `build_ds1307_with_full_date` — all 7 keys set; assert each
  register encodes correctly.
- `build_ds1307_preset_system` — `ds1307@0x68?preset=system`
  reads `std::time::SystemTime::now()` (or `chrono::Local::now()`),
  asserts the registers fall within a small window of "now" at
  test-run time (allow a few seconds of slack for test scheduling).
- `build_ds1307_preset_conflicts_with_hour` — `?preset=system&hour=12`
  rejected with the named error.
- `build_ds1307_out_of_range_rejected` — `?hour=24`, `?minute=60`,
  `?dow=0`, `?year=100` all rejected with named errors.
- `build_ds1307_unknown_param_still_rejected` — the existing
  `?temp=25.0` test passes unchanged (regression).

Plus an integration test in `tests/i2c.rs` or wherever existing
ds1307 end-to-end tests live: assemble the existing
`examples/i2c/tmp101/tmp101.lgo`-style fixture against
`ds1307@0x68?hour=12&minute=34&second=56`, run it through the
emulator, observe that an i2c-read of register 0x02 returns 0x12.

## Out of scope

- **No persistence in the emulator.** Battery-backed survival
  across runs is purely a web/UI concern — see "Battery story"
  below. The emulator stays stateless across CLI invocations;
  each `--i2c-device ds1307@...` call constructs a fresh device.
- **No 12-hour mode** on the Hours register. The device already
  stores 24-hour values per its doc; `?hour=` takes 0-23
  decimal and BCD-encodes it. Bit 6 (12/24 mode flag) stays 0.
- **No SQW/OUT control bit support.** Register 0x07 stays
  default-zero. Future brief if anyone needs the squarewave
  output simulated.
- **No `?control=<n>` param.** Skip until something actually
  reads register 0x07 meaningfully.
- **No `Ds1307Device::with_system_clock()` Rust API.** Use the
  registry path (`?preset=system`) for that. Library users who
  want it can call `with_initial_registers(...)` with a chrono-
  computed argument.

## Battery story (context for the param design — not work for this brief)

The web side will offer two toggles for the RTC live demo:

- **"No battery"** — emulator gets `ds1307@0x68` with no params;
  registers boot at 0:00:00. Each page reload starts fresh.
- **"With battery"** — emulator gets `ds1307@0x68?hour=X&minute=Y&second=Z`
  where X/Y/Z come from web localStorage:

  ```javascript
  // localStorage key: "ds1307.battery"
  // value: { set_value: { h, m, s, ... }, set_at_ms: <Date.now() at set> }
  const persisted = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("ds1307.battery"));
  const elapsed_s = (Date.now() - persisted.set_at_ms) / 1000;
  const effective_total_s = (persisted.set_value.h*3600 + persisted.set_value.m*60
                             + persisted.set_value.s) + elapsed_s;
  const effective = secondsToHms(effective_total_s % 86400);
  // build the --i2c-device spec from effective.{h,m,s} and reload the wasm bundle
  ```

This gives "battery survives page reload AND clock continues
ticking while the page was closed" with **zero emulator-side
persistence machinery** — the emulator just sees a constructed
device with the right initial values. dcemu does not need to
think about localStorage, chrono in the web, or page-reload
lifecycle. dwxas owns all of that.

The discipline the web side must enforce: whenever the assembler-
side set demo writes to the registers via i2c, the web must
update localStorage synchronously with the i2c-write transaction
completion. Otherwise next reload's "battery state" is stale.
That's a web-side concern, not an emulator concern.

## Naming rationale

Keep the param names device-shaped (`hour`, `minute`, `second`)
rather than feature-shaped (`battery`, `time_now`). Matches the
pattern already in use (`tmp101@0x4A?temp=25.0` doesn't pretend
to be a thermistor; `add1@0x50?wrap=10` doesn't pretend to be a
counter modulus). The web layer owns the battery metaphor.

## Downstream

After this lands and mike promotes `sw-cor24-emulator/main`:

- dcxas's `i2c_ds1307_read.s` demo gains a meaningful one-liner:
  `cor24-emu --lgo ds1307_read.lgo --i2c-device 'ds1307@0x68?preset=system'`
  prints the actual host time. Document in the demo's header
  comment (separate one-line follow-up PR in `sw-cor24-x-assembler`).
- dwxas can wire the battery toggle into the web demo without
  needing any new Rust API beyond what `Ds1307HandleExt` already
  provides for slider-driven mid-session updates.

## When done

`dg-mark-pr` to rename `feat/ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset`
→ `pr/ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset`. Mike relays via
`dg-relay dcemu sw-cor24-emulator pr/ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset`.
Standard two-pr saga pattern (optional `pr/...-saga-complete`
bookkeeping branch).

attachment.snippet:
1	# Brief: DS1307 initial-time CLI params + system-clock preset
2	
3	**Owner:** dcemu
4	**Branch:** `pr/ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset`
5	**Repo:** `sw-cor24-x-assembler`'s emulator path-dep — `sw-cor24-emulator`
6	**Drafted by:** dcxas (2026-05-16)
7	
8	## Why this brief exists
9	
10	dcxas just shipped two RTC demos in the assembler repo:
11	`i2c_ds1307_read.s` (passive observer, prints `HH:MM:SS\n`) and
12	`i2c_ds1307_set.s` (UART-driven setter that writes the registers
13	via i2c then reads them back). Both work end-to-end against
14	`cor24-emu --i2c-device ds1307@0x68` — but **the CLI spec accepts
15	no params**, so out-of-the-box the read demo prints `00:00:00`
16	forever. The only way to get a non-zero starting time today is
17	the in-Rust `Ds1307HandleExt::set_*` API (which the web panel
18	will use, but is unavailable to CLI users).
19	
20	This brief proposes the missing CLI surface so:
21	
22	- dcxas's read demo can show meaningful output from a one-liner
23	  without the web panel (`--i2c-device 'ds1307@0x68?preset=system'`
24	  shows the host clock).
25	- dwxas's `web-sw-cor24-x-assembler` battery-backed RTC demo
26	  (currently in design — see "Battery story" below) has a clean
27	  emulator-side hook to pass an effective initial time without
28	  routing through `handle.set_time`.
29	- The "no battery" vs "with battery" web toggle becomes a one-arg
30	  CLI-side difference rather than two separate Rust code paths.
31	
32	## What changes
33	
34	Add three things to `sw-cor24-emulator`:
35	
36	1. **`Ds1307Device::with_initial_registers(...)`** — a public
37	   constructor that takes the 8 BCD register values (S/M/H/DoW/
38	   Date/Month/Year/Control) and pre-loads them. Defaults match
39	   `new()` (all-zero, control = 0). Use it in `new()` itself for
40	   the existing "all-zero" path, so there's one source of truth.
41	2. **Registry parser support for `ds1307@<addr>?<params>`** — in
42	   `src/peripherals/i2c/registry.rs::build_i2c_device`'s `"ds1307"`
43	   arm, accept these param keys (all optional):
44	
45	   | Key      | Value         | Behavior                                  |
46	   |----------|---------------|-------------------------------------------|
47	   | `hour`   | 0-23 decimal  | Sets the Hours register (BCD-encoded; 24-hour). Conflicts with `preset`. |
48	   | `minute` | 0-59 decimal  | Sets Minutes. Conflicts with `preset`.     |
49	   | `second` | 0-59 decimal  | Sets Seconds. Conflicts with `preset`.     |
50	   | `date`   | 1-31 decimal  | Sets Date register. Conflicts with `preset`. |
51	   | `month`  | 1-12 decimal  | Sets Month. Conflicts with `preset`.       |
52	   | `year`   | 0-99 decimal  | Sets Year register (2-digit). Conflicts with `preset`. |
53	   | `dow`    | 1-7 decimal   | Sets DayOfWeek register. Conflicts with `preset`. |
54	   | `preset` | `system`      | Reads host clock at attach time and sets all 7 time registers from it. Conflicts with any of the above keys. |
55	
56	   Reject unknown keys (current pattern). Reject out-of-range
57	   values with a clear error message naming both the key and the
58	   value (`"ds1307 'hour' out of range: 24 (valid: 0-23)"`).
59	   Reject `preset` with `?hour=` etc. set
60	   (`"ds1307 'preset' and explicit register values are mutually exclusive"`).
61	
62	3. **Help text update** in the CLI:
63	
64	   ```
65	   --i2c-device <spec>    Attach an I2C device (repeatable). Specs:
66	                            add1@<addr>[?wrap=<n>]                universal +1 test slave
67	                            tmp101@<addr>[?temp=<f>][?config=<n>] TI temp sensor
68	                            ds1307@<addr>[?hour=<n>][?minute=<n>]
69	                                  [?second=<n>][?date=<n>][?month=<n>]
70	                                  [?year=<n>][?dow=<n>]
71	                                  [?preset=system]                Dallas/Maxim RTC
72	   ```
73	
74	That's the entire surface change. The existing
75	`Ds1307HandleExt::set_time / set_date / tick_second / ...` runtime
76	API stays as-is (it's what the web panel's slider will use for
77	mid-session updates).
78	
79	## Tests
80	
81	Add to `src/peripherals/i2c/registry.rs` test module:
82	
83	- `build_ds1307_with_time_params` — `ds1307@0x68?hour=12&minute=34&second=56`
84	  → device address 0x68, register 0x00 = 0x56 (BCD), 0x01 = 0x34,
85	  0x02 = 0x12.
86	- `build_ds1307_with_full_date` — all 7 keys set; assert each
87	  register encodes correctly.
88	- `build_ds1307_preset_system` — `ds1307@0x68?preset=system`
89	  reads `std::time::SystemTime::now()` (or `chrono::Local::now()`),
90	  asserts the registers fall within a small window of "now" at
91	  test-run time (allow a few seconds of slack for test scheduling).
92	- `build_ds1307_preset_conflicts_with_hour` — `?preset=system&hour=12`
93	  rejected with the named error.
94	- `build_ds1307_out_of_range_rejected` — `?hour=24`, `?minute=60`,
95	  `?dow=0`, `?year=100` all rejected with named errors.
96	- `build_ds1307_unknown_param_still_rejected` — the existing
97	  `?temp=25.0` test passes unchanged (regression).
98	
99	Plus an integration test in `tests/i2c.rs` or wherever existing
100	ds1307 end-to-end tests live: assemble the existing
101	`examples/i2c/tmp101/tmp101.lgo`-style fixture against
102	`ds1307@0x68?hour=12&minute=34&second=56`, run it through the
103	emulator, observe that an i2c-read of register 0x02 returns 0x12.
104	
105	## Out of scope
106	
107	- **No persistence in the emulator.** Battery-backed survival
108	  across runs is purely a web/UI concern — see "Battery story"
109	  below. The emulator stays stateless across CLI invocations;
110	  each `--i2c-device ds1307@...` call constructs a fresh device.
111	- **No 12-hour mode** on the Hours register. The device already
112	  stores 24-hour values per its doc; `?hour=` takes 0-23
113	  decimal and BCD-encodes it. Bit 6 (12/24 mode flag) stays 0.
114	- **No SQW/OUT control bit support.** Register 0x07 stays
115	  default-zero. Future brief if anyone needs the squarewave
116	  output simulated.
117	- **No `?control=<n>` param.** Skip until something actually
118	  reads register 0x07 meaningfully.
119	- **No `Ds1307Device::with_system_clock()` Rust API.** Use the
120	  registry path (`?preset=system`) for that. Library users who
121	  want it can call `with_initial_registers(...)` with a chrono-
122	  computed argument.
123	
124	## Battery story (context for the param design — not work for this brief)
125	
126	The web side will offer two toggles for the RTC live demo:
127	
128	- **"No battery"** — emulator gets `ds1307@0x68` with no params;
129	  registers boot at 0:00:00. Each page reload starts fresh.
130	- **"With battery"** — emulator gets `ds1307@0x68?hour=X&minute=Y&second=Z`
131	  where X/Y/Z come from web localStorage:
132	
133	  ```javascript
134	  // localStorage key: "ds1307.battery"
135	  // value: { set_value: { h, m, s, ... }, set_at_ms: <Date.now() at set> }
136	  const persisted = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("ds1307.battery"));
137	  const elapsed_s = (Date.now() - persisted.set_at_ms) / 1000;
138	  const effective_total_s = (persisted.set_value.h*3600 + persisted.set_value.m*60
139	                             + persisted.set_value.s) + elapsed_s;
140	  const effective = secondsToHms(effective_total_s % 86400);
141	  // build the --i2c-device spec from effective.{h,m,s} and reload the wasm bundle
142	  ```
143	
144	This gives "battery survives page reload AND clock continues
145	ticking while the page was closed" with **zero emulator-side
146	persistence machinery** — the emulator just sees a constructed
147	device with the right initial values. dcemu does not need to
148	think about localStorage, chrono in the web, or page-reload
149	lifecycle. dwxas owns all of that.
150	
151	The discipline the web side must enforce: whenever the assembler-
152	side set demo writes to the registers via i2c, the web must
153	update localStorage synchronously with the i2c-write transaction
154	completion. Otherwise next reload's "battery state" is stale.
155	That's a web-side concern, not an emulator concern.
156	
157	## Naming rationale
158

2	
3	**Owner:** dcxas

10	
11	## Cross-repo coordination
12	
13	This brief sits in the middle of a three-agent thread:
14	
15	- Upstream: [`dcemu-ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset.md`](dcemu-ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset.md)
16	  — adds `?hour=&minute=&second=...` and `?preset=system` CLI params
17	  to the emulator's `--i2c-device ds1307@...` spec, plus a
18	  `Ds1307Device::with_initial_registers(...)` constructor for the
19	  Rust API.
20	- Downstream: [`dwxas-battery-backed-rtc.md`](dwxas-battery-backed-rtc.md)
21	  — web "No battery / With battery" toggle on top of dcemu's
22	  constructor.
23	
24	Your demos sit between them: they're the artifact the user actually
25	runs to see the RTC behaviour. CLI users feed your `.s` files
26	through `cor24-emu --i2c-device 'ds1307@0x68?preset=system'`; web
27	users see them in the dropdown.
28	
29	## Part 1 — Unblock the set chain (do first; doesn't wait on dcemu)
30	
31	Your `pr/i2c-ds1307-set` and `pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete` are
32	blocked on a rename/rename conflict with the already-merged
33	`pr/i2c-ds1307-read` chain: both branches archive the same prior
34	saga to different timestamped paths under
35	`.agentrail-archive/i2c-examples-<TIMESTAMP>/`, plus a content
36	conflict on `tests/integration_tests.rs::examples()` from both
37	adding entries.
38	
39	Resolve by rebasing onto the current `origin/dev`. The rename/rename
40	will resolve to keeping the read chain's archive directory (already
41	on dev) and dropping set's redundant archive of the same source.
42	The integration_tests.rs conflict resolves by keeping the read entry
43	already on dev and inserting the set entry alphabetically after it.
44	
45	```bash
46	cd /disk1/.../work/dcxas/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-x-assembler
47	git fetch origin --prune
48	git switch dev && git merge --ff-only origin/dev
49	
50	for pr in pr/i2c-ds1307-set pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete; do
51	  git switch "$pr"
52	  git branch -m "$pr" "feat/${pr#pr/}"
53	  git rebase origin/dev
54	  # in the resolver:
55	  #   - for the .agentrail-archive/ renames: accept origin/dev's
56	  #     i2c-examples-<earlier-timestamp> directory; drop set's
57	  #     redundant archive of the same source paths.
58	  #   - for tests/integration_tests.rs: keep "I2C RTC Read"
59	  #     already on dev; insert "I2C RTC Set" alphabetically after it.
60	  # then git rebase --continue, then:
61	  git branch -m "feat/${pr#pr/}" "$pr"
62	done
63	```
64	
65	Then `dg-mark-pr` (or just leave the `pr/` name) to re-signal.
66	
67	## Part 2 — Quality consolidation (recommended, not blocking)
68	
69	For both the read pair and the set pair, the two-pr-branch pattern
70	diverged from the earlier shape (where `pr/<saga>-saga-complete` was
71	a strict superset of `pr/<saga>`). On `i2c-ds1307-read`, the `feat`
72	branch carried a follow-up "rename label to RTC" commit that the
73	`saga-complete` branch lacked; mike worked around it by relaying
74	saga-complete first and feat second so the rename auto-merged. The
75	set pair likely has the same shape.
76	
77	Going forward (the next saga is up to you), please restore the
78	earlier discipline: **the `saga-complete` branch should always be
79	a strict superset of the `feat` branch's tip at the moment you
80	signal both.** If you add a follow-up "fix(label)" commit after
81	signaling `feat`, the corresponding `saga-complete` must include it
82	too (rebase saga-complete on top of the new feat tip and re-signal
83	both).
84	
85	This is easier on the coordinator (no order-dependent merge
86	strategy needed) and avoids the rare case where the feat-only
87	commits are dropped silently.

# Brief: rebase `pr/i2c-ds1307-set`, refresh demo headers for `?preset=system`

**Owner:** dcxas
**Branches:**
- `pr/i2c-ds1307-set` (rebase)
- `pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete` (rebase)
- `pr/document-ds1307-cli-preset` (new, sequenced after dcemu ships)
**Repo:** `sw-cor24-x-assembler`
**Drafted by:** mike (2026-05-16)

## Cross-repo coordination

This brief sits in the middle of a three-agent thread:

- Upstream: [`dcemu-ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset.md`](dcemu-ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset.md)
  — adds `?hour=&minute=&second=...` and `?preset=system` CLI params
  to the emulator's `--i2c-device ds1307@...` spec, plus a
  `Ds1307Device::with_initial_registers(...)` constructor for the
  Rust API.
- Downstream: [`dwxas-battery-backed-rtc.md`](dwxas-battery-backed-rtc.md)
  — web "No battery / With battery" toggle on top of dcemu's
  constructor.

Your demos sit between them: they're the artifact the user actually
runs to see the RTC behaviour. CLI users feed your `.s` files
through `cor24-emu --i2c-device 'ds1307@0x68?preset=system'`; web
users see them in the dropdown.

## Part 1 — Unblock the set chain (do first; doesn't wait on dcemu)

Your `pr/i2c-ds1307-set` and `pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete` are
blocked on a rename/rename conflict with the already-merged
`pr/i2c-ds1307-read` chain: both branches archive the same prior
saga to different timestamped paths under
`.agentrail-archive/i2c-examples-<TIMESTAMP>/`, plus a content
conflict on `tests/integration_tests.rs::examples()` from both
adding entries.

Resolve by rebasing onto the current `origin/dev`. The rename/rename
will resolve to keeping the read chain's archive directory (already
on dev) and dropping set's redundant archive of the same source.
The integration_tests.rs conflict resolves by keeping the read entry
already on dev and inserting the set entry alphabetically after it.

```bash
cd /disk1/.../work/dcxas/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-x-assembler
git fetch origin --prune
git switch dev && git merge --ff-only origin/dev

for pr in pr/i2c-ds1307-set pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete; do
  git switch "$pr"
  git branch -m "$pr" "feat/${pr#pr/}"
  git rebase origin/dev
  # in the resolver:
  #   - for the .agentrail-archive/ renames: accept origin/dev's
  #     i2c-examples-<earlier-timestamp> directory; drop set's
  #     redundant archive of the same source paths.
  #   - for tests/integration_tests.rs: keep "I2C RTC Read"
  #     already on dev; insert "I2C RTC Set" alphabetically after it.
  # then git rebase --continue, then:
  git branch -m "feat/${pr#pr/}" "$pr"
done
```

Then `dg-mark-pr` (or just leave the `pr/` name) to re-signal.

## Part 2 — Quality consolidation (recommended, not blocking)

For both the read pair and the set pair, the two-pr-branch pattern
diverged from the earlier shape (where `pr/<saga>-saga-complete` was
a strict superset of `pr/<saga>`). On `i2c-ds1307-read`, the `feat`
branch carried a follow-up "rename label to RTC" commit that the
`saga-complete` branch lacked; mike worked around it by relaying
saga-complete first and feat second so the rename auto-merged. The
set pair likely has the same shape.

Going forward (the next saga is up to you), please restore the
earlier discipline: **the `saga-complete` branch should always be
a strict superset of the `feat` branch's tip at the moment you
signal both.** If you add a follow-up "fix(label)" commit after
signaling `feat`, the corresponding `saga-complete` must include it
too (rebase saga-complete on top of the new feat tip and re-signal
both).

This is easier on the coordinator (no order-dependent merge
strategy needed) and avoids the rare case where the feat-only
commits are dropped silently.

## Part 3 — Refresh demo headers (wait for dcemu to ship)

After mike promotes `sw-cor24-emulator/main` with the
`?preset=system` and explicit-register params:

### `examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_read.s` header

Add to the existing usage block:

```
; CLI one-liners (post-dcemu/ds1307-initial-time-and-system-preset):
;
;   # show host clock (registry preset reads SystemTime::now()):
;   cor24-asm src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_read.s -o /tmp/r.lgo
;   cor24-emu --lgo /tmp/r.lgo --i2c-device 'ds1307@0x68?preset=system'
;
;   # show a specific time:
;   cor24-emu --lgo /tmp/r.lgo \
;       --i2c-device 'ds1307@0x68?hour=12&minute=34&second=56'
;
; Without any params, the device boots at 00:00:00 — the demo prints
; that and increments from there.
```

### `examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s` header

Note that the set demo is now redundant with `?preset=system` for
the "show host clock" use case (no UART input needed). Keep the
set demo — it remains the way to demonstrate i2c writes to live
registers — but add a line in the header pointing CLI users at
`?preset=system` if all they want is the time displayed:

```
; If you just want to see host time without typing it in via UART,
; use scripts/run-demo.sh i2c-ds1307-read with --preset=system
; (or the dropdown's 'I2C RTC Read' on the web demo). This setter
; demo is for cases where you want to demonstrate i2c writes to
; the RTC registers.
```

### Web dropdown labels

No change. The existing labels (`I2C RTC Read`, `I2C RTC Set`)
already match the device class (RTC), not the chip (DS1307) or the
feature (battery). Keep that convention.

## Acceptance

**Part 1**: both set branches relay cleanly via `dg-relay` (no
conflict aborts) after the rebases.

**Part 3**: demo header comments updated as above, single commit
"docs(examples): document --i2c-device ds1307 CLI params in demo
headers" on `pr/document-ds1307-cli-preset`. No `.s` source
changes; no test changes; no dropdown rename. Cross-link the
upstream brief in the commit body.

## Out of scope

- **No new RTC demo `.s` files.** The two existing demos cover
  read and write; dwxas's battery toggle handles the cross-reload
  scenario at the web layer.
- **No `Ds1307HandleExt` changes in this repo.** That API lives
  in `sw-cor24-emulator` and stays as-is.
- **No "battery" naming creeping into demos.** The web layer owns
  that metaphor; demos remain device-shaped.

# Saga: i2c-ds1307-set (rebuild)

## Goal

Add `src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s` — interactive UART-
driven DS1307 RTC time-set demo. Same content as the previous
attempt; this saga rebuilds the branch chain off current `dev`
(which already has `i2c-ds1307-read` merged) per
`dcxas-finish-ds1307-set-and-document-cli-preset.md` Part 1.

The original `pr/i2c-ds1307-set` and `pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete`
had a rename/rename conflict against dev: both branches archived
`i2c-examples` to different timestamped directories (T141431 from
read vs T142625 from set), plus a `tests/integration_tests.rs`
content conflict because both inserted "I2C RTC X" at the same
alphabetical slot. Rebuild resolves both by archiving the
NOW-active saga on dev (`i2c-ds1307-read`) instead of redundantly
re-archiving `i2c-examples`, and by inserting "I2C RTC Set"
after the already-merged "I2C RTC Read".

## What's actually changing

Same as the prior attempt:

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/examples/assembler/i2c_ds1307_set.s` (new) | UART RX → 6-digit ASCII parse → BCD → i2c-write registers 0-2 → i2c-read back → print "HH:MM:SS\n". Identical to the file we already verified works (saved to /tmp/i2c_ds1307_set.s before deleting the stale pr/ branches). |
| `tests/integration_tests.rs` (2 hunks) | register `("I2C RTC Set", ...)` alphabetically AFTER `"I2C RTC Read"` (now merged); add `"I2C RTC Set"` to `non_halting` in `test_all_examples_halt`. Display name uses RTC (device class) not DS1307 (chip) from the start — no follow-up rename commit needed. |

## Saga-complete supersets-feat discipline (Part 2)

The work commit will use the final "I2C RTC Set" label from the
start. No follow-up "rename label" commit. `pr/i2c-ds1307-set-
saga-complete` will branch off `pr/i2c-ds1307-set` AFTER `dg-mark-
pr`, so it's a strict superset (= feat + 1 bookkeeping commit).
Restores the earlier discipline per mike's brief Part 2.

## Out of scope

- No new RTC demo .s files beyond the existing two.
- No emulator changes (waits on dcemu's parallel work).
- No demo header updates for `?preset=system` (Part 3 of mike's
  brief — waits on dcemu to ship).
- No "battery" naming.

## Steps

1. **i2c-ds1307-set** — single step. Restore the .s, register in
   tests, verify end-to-end with the same three test inputs that
   passed last time (12:34:56, 09:30:00, 23:59:59), commit.

## When done

`dg-mark-pr` → `pr/i2c-ds1307-set`. Then `feat/i2c-ds1307-set-
saga-complete` off `pr/i2c-ds1307-set`, commit bookkeeping,
`dg-mark-pr` → `pr/i2c-ds1307-set-saga-complete`. Both branches
relay cleanly without rebase next time per Part 2 discipline.

```
feat(examples): add I2C RTC Set demo (UART-driven time set)

Reads 6 ASCII digits (HHMMSS) from UART RX, parses to BCD,
writes the DS1307 seconds/minutes/hours registers via i2c, then
reads back and prints HH:MM:SS\n. Halt.

Pairs with i2c_ds1307_read.s (already merged): read = observer
that prints the current registers; set = driver that proves the
i2c write path through the same bit-bang library. Together they
exercise both directions of the DS1307 protocol.

Display label uses the device class ("I2C RTC Set") per the
dropdown convention; the chip name (DS1307) stays in code
comments and the filename.

Registered in tests/integration_tests.rs::examples() alphabetically
after "I2C RTC Read", and added to non_halting (uart_getc busy-
polls UART RX forever without input).

to resolve the rename/rename conflict where the old chain had a
redundant archive of i2c-examples. Per mike's brief
dcxas-finish-ds1307-set-and-document-cli-preset.md Part 1.
```
